text:
Market today:
Inflation eased more than expected in June, driven partly by falling oil prices. This is generally good news for your tech and chip-heavy portfolio, as it could reduce pressure on the Fed.

Your holdings:
SOXL is down almost 30% and SPAL over 32%, both leveraged ETFs with "SELL/NEW" status. These are your biggest concerns, especially on margin, which amplifies losses. Your SMH position is also down 5.69%.

Watch:
Keep an eye on continued inflation data for signs of sustained easing. Also, watch the chip sector; it's getting attention, even from Cramer.

Bottom line:
Prioritize risk management on your leveraged, underperforming positions while watching for sustained macro tailwinds for tech.
plays: SOXL: Consider trimming or respecting the 200 stop. This leveraged ETF is a significant drag at -29.8%.
SPAL: Watch the 21.38 stop closely. This new, leveraged position is down over 32% and moves fast.
NVDA: Consider a new buy at 208.32-208.11 with a stop at 207.28, supported by strong scanner signals and sector interest.
